pull.title: ci: use LLVM/Clang 22 in tidy job
pull.body:
Changes needed for moving to Clang 22 in the tidy job.

Based on #34422.

events.body: The following sections might be updated with supplementary metadata relevant to reviewers and maintainers. ### Reviews See [the guideline](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md#code-review) for information on the review process. | Type | Reviewers | | ---- | --------- | | Concept ACK | [hebasto](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34662#pullrequestreview-3848017413) | If your review is incorrectly listed, please copy-paste <!--meta-tag:bot-skip--> into the comment that the bot should ignore. ### Conflicts Reviewers, this pull request conflicts with the following ones: * [#34672](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34672) (mining: add reason/debug to `submitSolution` and unify with `submitBlock` by w0xlt) * [#34644](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/pull/34644) (mining: add submitBlock to IPC Mining interface by w0xlt) If you consider this pull request important, please also help to review the conflicting pull requests. Ideally, start with the one that should be merged first.

tree 4988d508e9a45f29abe96eeee087a00fd6d515b7
parent 7c8030143925b3ed8ca74b9e616dca20d36c4440
author Ryan Ofsky <ryan@ofsky.org> 1768397857 -0500
committer fanquake <fanquake@gmail.com> 1772038928 +0000

ipc, test: Add tests for unclean disconnect and thread busy behavior

Upcoming libmultiprocess changes are expected to alter this behavior
(https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues/34250#issuecomment-3749243782),
making test coverage useful for documenting current behavior and validating the
intended changes.

test: Updates needed after bitcoin-core/libmultiprocess#240

Upstream PR bitcoin-core/libmultiprocess#240 fixed various issues which require
updates to python IPC tests. Those changes are made in this commit.

tree 95fdd5bb838329367087dc0614215983650dcbba
parent 8386fadd9ee4f56cb6c4675dc87ab057999f7d6b
author fanquake <fanquake@gmail.com> 1771937788 +0000
committer fanquake <fanquake@gmail.com> 1772038942 +0000

tidy: clang-tidy is required

Otherwise this check will "pass", like:
```bash
-- Detecting CXX compile features - done
-- Found LLVM 21.1.8
-- Found clang-tidy: CLANG_TIDY_EXE-NOTFOUND
-- Configuring done (0.5s)
```

events.message:
tidy: clang-tidy is required

Otherwise this check will "pass", like:
```bash

-- Found LLVM 21.1.8
-- Found clang-tidy: CLANG_TIDY_EXE-NOTFOUND
-- Configuring done (0.5s)
```

author fanquake <fanquake@gmail.com> 1771928420 +0000
committer fanquake <fanquake@gmail.com> 1772038942 +0000

tidy: remove deprecated header

```bash
   15 | #warning The ClangTidyModuleRegistry.h header is deprecated and will be removed in LLVM 24. All of the symbols it used to define have been moved into ClangTidyModule.h.
      |  ^~~~~~~
[100%] Linking CXX shared module libbitcoin-tidy.so
```

interpreter: remove clang-tidy suppression

comments.body: cc @ryanofsky @sjors Looking at the multiprocess source, it seems we are already suppressing this in at least one other place, do we just want to suppress it here too? See also `bugprone-unused-return-value` below. If source changes are needed in libmultiprocess, we could get them in as part of #34422.

In commit "f: libmultiprocess tidy errors" (5be60409931f572d6b094a351e993f646caee02e)

Can this be changed to `(void)client_connection.release();` to see if it fixes the tidy error? `(void)` should be a more typical way of indicating the return value is intentionally not used
Also added this one, since it's in a test anyway.
Let me know if that works and I'll switch it over in https://github.com/bitcoin-core/libmultiprocess/pull/244
Looks like using `(void)` is fine here.
